title: nepeta betonicifolia subsp. strictifolia
intro: Nepeta betonicifolia subsp. strictifolia, also known as strict-leaved catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta betonicifolia plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. The health benefits of Nepeta betonicifolia subsp. strictifolia include its ability to reduce fever, calm the nervous system, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a useful treatment for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as insomnia, anxiety, and respiratory issues.
